rationale.R1.1: The section explicitly stakes a 'central contribution' claim (unified evaluation framework, deployment-gap metric, commitment reliability frontier); assessing whether that claim is well-scoped, non-circular, and adequately distinguished from its components is a standard expectation of any section review.
rationale.R1.2: The section introduces formal structure—the p(t)=c(t)−d(t) decomposition, two-level planner/dispatcher architecture, the fixed-vs-learned weight dichotomy, and the O(NT)→O(KM) complexity reduction argument—each of which carries internal logical commitments that must be checked for coherence and completeness.
rationale.R1.5: Five topically distinct paragraphs (problem formalization, architecture, cost model, contribution framing, complexity approximation) are presented in sequence without explicit transitional logic; narrative ordering and whether the complexity discussion belongs in this section rather than a methods section are natural review targets.
rationale.R1.6: Several inline issues are reviewable from the text alone: undefined notation introduced without declaration (K, M, N, T first appear mid-flow), the phrase 'buy-sell spread common to all three markets' presupposes context not given in this section, and passive constructions reduce precision throughout.
